reasoning.overall: Bayern Munich travel to Leverkusen as heavy market favorites (odds ~1.50) after clinching their 35th Bundesliga title and winning 14 of their last 15 matches across all competitions, including a 4-2 win over Stuttgart. However, Leverkusen held them to a 1-1 draw back in mid-March and Bayern hasn't won at Leverkusen in the league since October 2021. Leverkusen come in on the back of a disappointing 2-1 defeat to Augsburg but have strong cup pedigree. Bayern are missing Gnabry (adductor), Ulreich, Karl and Bischof (muscle injuries), while Leverkusen will not have Arthur and Martin Terrier. Bayern's treble pursuit and superior squad depth favor them, but Leverkusen's home fortress vs Bayern and Kompany's focus on the PSG CL semi next week keep this competitive.
reasoning.t1_result: Market implies ~67% Bayern but Leverkusen's remarkable home record vs Bayern and possible rotation for Bayern ahead of PSG pulls probability toward a narrower Bayern edge. A 1-2 or 1-1 outcome is most likely given recent head-to-heads producing low-to-medium totals.
reasoning.t2_player: Bayern's expected XI is Neuer; Laimer, Upamecano, Tah, Davies; Kimmich, Pavlovic; Olise, Musiala, Diaz; Kane in a 4-2-3-1. Leverkusen likely in 3-4-2-1: Flekken; Quansah, Bade, Tapsoba; Vazquez, Palacios, Garcia, Grimaldo; Maza, Tella; Schick. Kane is top scorer candidate with 6 goals in 4 cup games this season.
reasoning.t3_events: Bayern tend to score in both halves (70% of matches). Expect a Bayern opener around 25-60' with Kane or Diaz; Leverkusen most dangerous on set pieces via Grimaldo. Multiple yellow cards likely given Zwayer as referee and H2H history of confrontations.
reasoning.t4_stats: Bayern averages 65% possession, 19 shots, 8 on target under Kompany. Leverkusen at home generates solid shot volume but may cede possession. Expect Bayern 58-62% possession, 15-17 shots to Leverkusen's 10-12, with both teams showing high pass accuracy.

reasoning.overall: Bayern enter this semi-final in devastating form (9W-1D-0L in last 10, including two wins over Real Madrid and a 4-2 at Stuttgart) having just clinched the Bundesliga title, while Leverkusen are inconsistent (3W-5D-2L last 10, losing at Augsburg mid-week and exiting the CL picture). Head-to-head this season favors Bayern: a 3-0 home win in November and a chaotic 1-1 draw at the BayArena in March where Bayern were severely depleted yet still held on. Bayern's Kane-Olise-Musiala-Díaz attack is the most productive front line in Europe right now; Leverkusen can hurt them via Grimaldo set-pieces, Schick, Ben Seghir and Tillman, and crowd support at the BayArena gives them a real chance. Tactically Leverkusen's higher line plus Bayern's vertical speed through Olise/Díaz is a dangerous matchup for the hosts, but knockout cup football at home, plus Bayern potentially rotating after the Madrid CL exertions, keeps the draw/home share meaningful. Net: Bayern favorites but not overwhelming given venue and Leverkusen's capacity to score.
reasoning.t1_result: Bayern's squad-wide superiority and elite form make them the clear favorite (~50%), but Leverkusen's home advantage in a one-off cup tie and their ability to score against anyone keeps the home win around 28% and draw around 22%. A 1-2 or 2-1 scoreline is plausible; 1-2 is modal because Bayern's attacking output is currently the league's best.
reasoning.t2_player: Leverkusen line up in their usual 3-4-2-1 with Flekken in goal, Tapsoba-Badé-Quansah at the back, Grimaldo/Vázquez as wing-backs, García-Andrich double pivot, Tillman & Ben Seghir behind Schick. Bayern in 4-2-3-1: Urbig (Neuer rotation possible but Urbig has been the cup keeper); Laimer-Upamecano-Tah-Stanišić; Kimmich-Pavlović; Olise-Musiala-Díaz; Kane. Kane, Olise, Díaz and Musiala are the primary goal threats; Schick and Grimaldo are Leverkusen's main outlets.
reasoning.t3_events: Expect an open, high-tempo match given both teams' pressing styles and the previous chaotic meetings (a red card each last time). Bayern typically score early via quick transitions; Leverkusen score from set pieces and second-half pressure. Several yellows likely (Andrich, Upamecano, Kimmich card-prone), one late sub wave from both benches, and a realistic chance of extra time given cup context.
reasoning.t4_stats: Bayern should dominate possession (~56-58%) and passing accuracy (~87% vs 83%). Shot counts closer than the narrative suggests because Leverkusen play open at home (Bayern 15 vs Leverkusen 12, SOT 6-4). Corners lean Bayern slightly; fouls even around 12 each; saves 3-4 per keeper.

reasoning.overall: Bayer Leverkusen and Bayern München face off in a highly anticipated DFB Pokal semi-final. Leverkusen's tactical discipline under Xabi Alonso and home advantage at the BayArena make them formidable, but Bayern's attacking firepower and depth give them a slight edge. Recent form suggests a tight, high-scoring affair, with Bayern likely edging it out or pushing it to extra time.
reasoning.t1_result: Bayern München are slight favorites due to their squad depth and experience in high-stakes matches, but Leverkusen's home form makes a draw in 90 minutes highly probable.
reasoning.t2_player: Harry Kane and Florian Wirtz will be key figures. Bayern will likely deploy a 4-2-3-1, while Leverkusen sticks to their 3-4-2-1.
reasoning.t3_events: Expect goals in the second half as fatigue sets in. Cards are likely in the midfield battle.
reasoning.t4_stats: Bayern to edge possession, but Leverkusen will be efficient on the counter. High shot counts for both.

sources.title: Preview: Bayer Leverkusen vs Bayern München
Match Preview: Bayer Leverkusen vs Bayern Munich

reasoning.overall: Bayern Munich enter this DFB Pokal semi-final in scintillating form, having recently secured the Bundesliga title and riding a 10-match unbeaten streak (9W 1D). Leverkusen, meanwhile, have been inconsistent, recently losing to Augsburg and struggling to turn draws into wins. Although Leverkusen held Bayern to a 1-1 draw at the BayArena in March, Bayern's overwhelming attacking firepower and momentum make them clear favorites to advance to the final.
reasoning.t1_result: Bayern are heavily favored to win in regular time given their dominant form and Leverkusen's recent struggles. A 1-2 or 1-3 away victory is highly probable, though a draw leading to extra time cannot be completely ruled out in a cup setting.
reasoning.t2_player: Bayern will likely field their strongest XI with Kane leading the line, supported by Musiala, Olise, and Gnabry. Leverkusen will rely on Schick, Terrier, and Grimaldo's creativity to counter-attack.
reasoning.t3_events: Bayern are expected to dominate possession and create early chances. Leverkusen might score on the break, but Bayern's sustained pressure should yield multiple goals. Expect a few yellow cards as Leverkusen tries to disrupt Bayern's rhythm.
reasoning.t4_stats: Bayern should control around 55-60% possession, generating more shots and corners. Leverkusen will have lower pass accuracy due to Bayern's pressing and will likely record more fouls and defensive actions.

reasoning.overall: Bayern enter as slight favourites on squad ceiling, deeper elite attacking options and stronger control numbers, but Leverkusen’s home edge, familiar automatisms and transitional threat keep this close. Recent meetings between these sides are often tactically dense rather than open blowouts, and the cup semi-final context should sharpen risk management early. Leverkusen are most dangerous when Wirtz can receive between Bayern’s midfield and back line and release Frimpong or Boniface/Schick into channels, while Bayern’s best route is sustained territory through Kimmich-Musiala combinations and wide isolation for Sané/Olise against aggressive wing-backs. With both teams carrying enough quality to score and enough structure to avoid chaos for long stretches, a narrow Bayern win is marginally more likely than a draw or home win.
reasoning.t1_result: The away side’s baseline level and chance-creation depth warrant the highest win probability, but not by a large margin because BayArena reduces the gap and Leverkusen have the tools to exploit Bayern in transition. A 1-2 away win is the single most likely scoreline while draws and one-goal home wins retain meaningful mass.
reasoning.t2_player: Leverkusen are projected in their usual back-three shape with Wirtz as the central creator and Frimpong/Grimaldo supplying width; Bayern are projected in a 4-2-3-1 built around Kane dropping, Musiala carrying and wide forwards attacking half-spaces. Kane, Musiala and Wirtz dominate scorer/MOTM probabilities because they are the most consistent final-third hubs in this matchup.
reasoning.t3_events: Expect a cautious first 20 minutes, then more transition-heavy spells once one side breaks the press. Tactical fouls in midfield and bookings for defenders dealing with pacey wide runners are plausible, with most substitutions arriving from 60 minutes onward as intensity rises.
reasoning.t4_stats: Bayern are slightly likelier to edge possession and passing accuracy through longer settled phases, but Leverkusen should still post double-digit shots because their pressing and wing-back progression generate entries. The save counts imply both teams create enough on-target efforts for the goalkeepers to be involved without forecasting a shot avalanche.

reasoning.overall: Bayern come in with a much stronger recent run (9 wins and 1 draw in 10, including elite-level Champions League results), while Leverkusen have been more volatile and recently lost at Augsburg. The March league meeting finished 1-1, showing Leverkusen can compete at BayArena, but Bayern’s current attacking level, squad depth, and superior individual quality through Kane, Musiala and Olise tilt the tie away from the hosts. Leverkusen still carry threat through Schick, Grimaldo and Palacios, especially in transition and set pieces, so a home goal is quite plausible, but Bayern’s midfield control and cleaner final-third execution make them slight-to-clear favorites in 90 minutes and stronger favorites to advance overall.
reasoning.t1_result: Leverkusen’s home edge and prior 1-1 head-to-head keep the draw live, but Bayern’s form and firepower justify the highest away probability. A narrow Bayern win such as 1-2 fits both teams’ scoring profiles and the knockout tension better than a blowout.
reasoning.t2_player: Leverkusen are projected in a back three/five structure to protect central zones and free Grimaldo high on the left, with Schick as the focal point. Bayern’s likely shape centers on Kimmich and Pavlović controlling buildup behind Musiala and Olise supplying Kane, which creates the clearest scorer probabilities for Bayern’s front line.
reasoning.t3_events: Expected pattern is a competitive first half, with Bayern enjoying longer possession spells and Leverkusen looking for direct breaks or set-piece entries. Given the stakes and the recent chaotic meeting, several tactical fouls and late attacking substitutions are likely, especially if the match is within one goal after the hour.
reasoning.t4_stats: Bayern should edge possession and pass accuracy because of their midfield structure, while Leverkusen’s home aggression keeps shot volume respectable. The projected save counts reflect both sides generating chances, with Leverkusen’s goalkeeper likely busier overall.

reasoning.overall: Chelsea project as the stronger side on squad quality, depth and likely control of territory, but Brighton at the Amex remain dangerous because of their aggressive build-up, wide rotations and ability to create transition moments against teams that press high. The matchup points to Chelsea having more possession and higher shot volume, yet Brighton's home edge and attacking talent make a clean away win less certain than raw talent suggests. Head-to-head trends in recent seasons have often produced open games, and key attackers on both sides are capable of deciding it, so the forecast leans Chelsea narrowly rather than heavily.
reasoning.t1_result: Chelsea get the largest win share because their baseline level and depth are higher, but Brighton's home advantage and chance creation keep draw and home-win probabilities meaningful. A one-goal away win and a scoring draw are the most plausible outcomes, with 1-2 narrowly ahead of 1-1.
reasoning.t2_player: Both teams are likely to use familiar structures: Brighton with a fluid back four and mobile front line, Chelsea with a possession-oriented 4-2-3-1/4-3-3 shape. The most likely scorers come from central forwards and advanced wide attackers who dominate box touches and set-piece involvement.
reasoning.t3_events: The game should start with Chelsea controlling the ball and Brighton looking to jump passing lanes before breaking quickly. Most substitutions are likely after 60 minutes as intensity drops, and the card risk is concentrated in full-backs and holding midfielders dealing with transition fouls.
reasoning.t4_stats: Chelsea are projected to edge possession, pass accuracy and total shots, while Brighton should still generate enough attacks at home to keep the shot count competitive. Saves and defensive actions are slightly higher for Brighton because they are more likely to spend longer without the ball.
